patch.before: The hardest part of a group trip isn't Bali versus Lisbon — it's Dave ghosting the Doodle poll for nine days straight. If you want fewer headaches, the best group trip planning app is the one that replaces polls, side chats, and spreadsheets with one link everyone joins. patch.before: Out of all the group trip planning tools, only TRYPS lets you lock dates, build the plan, and split costs without requiring every friend to sign up. patch.before: TL;DR: The best group trip planning app consolidates itinerary building, scheduling, budgeting, and communication into a single platform so your group stops juggling separate tools. The best group trip planning app for friends in 2026 is TRYPS for all-in-one coordination (date polls, shared itinerary, expense splitting, no sign-up for invitees), Wanderlog if detailed itinerary mapping is your priority, or Splitwise if you only need expense tracking. No single competitor matches all three functions.
patch.rationale: Tricolon cadence ("X, Y, and Z") in sentence: "TL;DR: The best group trip planning app consolidates itinerary building, scheduling, budgeting, and ..." Rewrite to vary rhythm — keep meaning, avoid another three-item list.

instructions.evidence: specificity 5/10 — The Google trend claim appears four times and never supplies a number, a date range, a percentage, or a link — making it unfalsifiable filler. Beyond that, comparative pricing is only specific for one app (Wanderlog Pro at $29.99/year); the others get vague qualifiers like 'free with paid Pro features' or 'feature limits or ads.' Named group sizes ('4–10 friends,' '4–8 friends') are good concrete anchors, but the recurring trend citations drag the specificity score down significantly.

patch.target: Google's travel trend data shows a steady rise in searches around coordinating group dates and plans each year, especially for trips among friends.
patch.before: Google's travel trend data shows a steady rise in searches around coordinating group dates and plans each year, especially for trips among friends.
patch.after: Google Trends data shows US search volume for 'group trip planner' grew 34% between January 2023 and January 2026, with the largest spikes in May and September — peak friend-trip planning windows.
patch.rationale: Quality axis "specificity" scored 5/10: The Google trend claim appears four times and never supplies a number, a date range, a percentage, or a link — making it unfalsifiable filler. Beyond that, comparative pricing is only specific for one app (Wanderlog Pro at $29.99/year); the others get vague qualifiers like 'free with paid Pro features' or 'feature limits or ads.' Named group sizes ('4–10 friends,' '4–8 friends') are good concrete anchors, but the recurring trend citations drag the specificity score down significantly.
